articles.title: ICICI Bank share price: Motilal Oswal sees 41% upside. Should you buy?
articles.summary: Brokerage firm Motilal Oswal Services has projected a potential upside of 41% for ICICI Bank, has maintained a ‘buy’ rating on the stock and set a target price of ₹1,750 per share.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Meesho shares sink for eighth consecutive session in longest post-listing sell-off. What’s driving the decline?
articles.summary: Meesho's shares continue to decline, dropping 5% to ₹166.60, wiping out 16.6% of its value. Lock-in expiry on June 9 may lead to significant selling pressure from early investors.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: SMR Jewels IPO allotment to be finalised today. Here's GMP, how to check status
articles.summary: According to platforms tracking the unofficial market, the grey market premium (GMP) for the SMR Jewels IPO is currently nil. Here's allotment status, latest GMP and IPO details.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Equity, Gold or Fixed Income: How should you invest amid stock market volatility? Here's a 50-40-10 strategy by MOSL
articles.summary: On precious metals, the firm maintains a neutral allocation stance on both gold and silver, while assigning greater weight to gold. The preference for gold is supported by continued buying from central banks and easing speculative excesses in global gold markets.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Maruti Suzuki share price gains after it unveils Wagon R as India’s first flex-fuel vehicle
articles.summary: Maruti Suzuki share price has fallen over 3% in one month and has declined 7% in three months. The auto stock has dropped 21% on a year-to-date (YTD) basis, while it has risen 8% in one year. Maruti Suzuki stock price has gained 38% in three years.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Vahh Chemicals IPO: Issue subscribed 77% so far on first bidding day. Check GMP, issue details
articles.summary: The Vahh Chemicals IPO opened for subscription on Thursday, 4 June and will close on Monday, 8 June. The SME issue is priced at ₹60 per equity share, with a face value of ₹10 per share. Vahh Chemicals IPO GMP is ₹11.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Top 10 small-cap mutual funds that gave up to 14% returns in 2026 so far
articles.summary: For the year, the Nifty Smallcap 100 index is up 2.46% and remains in the green for the third straight month, showing signs of resilience. April was the best month for the index with an 18% rise. The index added 0.73% in May and is flat with a positive bias in June so far.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Bond yields soften ahead of RBI policy; how should debt market investors position amid repo rate hike fears?
articles.summary: Indian rupee has depreciated by more than 5%, while the the benchmark 10-year bond yield has risen 34 bps since the US-Israeli war on Iran began on February 28.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: RBI MPC meeting: Here's the date, time, and where to watch Governor Sanjay Malhotra's address live on June 5
articles.summary: The six-member Monetary Policy Committee (MPC), led by RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra, commenced its three-day deliberations on June 3 and is scheduled to reveal its policy outcome at 10 a.m. on June 5.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Merritronix IPO share allotment likely today: Latest GMP, steps to check allotment status online for SME IPO
articles.summary: Investors can check the Merritronix IPO allotment status online either through the BSE website or via Bigshare Services, the registrar to the issue.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Inox Wind's stumble may be setting the stage for a strategic transition
articles.summary: Weak earnings, muted order inflows and missed guidance hurt sentiment, but a shift toward equipment supply contracts and captive demand could support Inox Wind's turnaround.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Hexagon Nutrition IPO opens June 5: 10 key things to know before subscribing; check GMP today
articles.summary: The company, which aims to raise ₹139 crore from the issue, has fixed a price band of ₹42-45 per share. The IPO will be open from 5 June to 9 June.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Will India’s economy be in trouble if IMD’s monsoon forecast proves accurate?
articles.summary: With the IMD predicting the weakest monsoon in over a decade, economists warn that the threat to growth hinges on specific regional risks, not just the headline numbers.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Physicswallah share price jumps 18% after partnership with NBFCs for student financing
articles.summary: PhysicsWallah share price surged 18% as it shifts to partnering with NBFCs for student lending, aiming to reduce balance sheet exposure and credit risks. The company will connect students with compliant lending partners to enhance affordability and scalability.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: NSE SME stock Simca Advertising jumps despite muted trends on Dalal Street
articles.summary: Simca Advertising share price opened at ₹134.50 apiece today, as compared to previous close of ₹136.15 on Wednesday.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Rajesh Exports under Sebi lens: ₹770 crore of LIC, retail investors' money at risk in this small-cap stock
articles.summary: In the last six years, the number of shareholders of Rajesh Exports has increased substantially from 22,472 in March 2020 to 198,796 as of March 2026. Marquee names like LIC also own a substantial stake in the firm, along with 194,110 retail investors.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Reliance Industries dividend record date tomorrow: Last chance to buy Mukesh Ambani-led stock
articles.summary: Reliance Industries has set June 5 as the record date for its final dividend of ₹6 per share for FY26, making June 4 the last day for investors to buy the stock and qualify for the payout.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Balrampur Chini vs Dalmia Bharat vs Dhampur Sugar vs Shree Renuka Sugar: Which sugar stock to buy after Q4 results 2026?
articles.summary: Sugar firms faced a tough earnings report for the fourth quarter of FY26, as many companies faced pressure on profits due to rising costs, declining sugar prices, and challenges in their ethanol and distillery sectors.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: India Money Market-Volumes Jump to Record as Bank Lending Booms
articles.summary: India’s money-market turnover jumped to a record as state-owned lenders stepped up borrowing to fund booming credit demand.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Cement price hikes miss the mark as cost inflation struggle intensifies
articles.summary: Stocks of large cement makers have declined 6-23% so far in 2026, with Adani Group companies Ambuja Cements and ACC falling more than 20% each
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Traders Brace for Big Yen Swings on BOJ, Intervention Risks
articles.summary: Options traders are ramping up hedges against sharp yen swings over the next two weeks, positioning for the Bank of Japan’s policy meeting and the possibility of currency intervention by authorities seeking to support the yen.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Nifty 50 EPS cuts: Cipla, L&T, Adani Enterprises, Adani Ports among top downgrades in May
articles.summary: In May 2026, 31 Nifty 50 companies — representing 62% of the index constituents — witnessed downgrades to their FY27 EPS estimates, while only 15 companies saw upgrades.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: TCS, Wipro to Infosys: IT stocks continue to fall amid AI jitters
articles.summary: TCS, Wipro to Infosys: IT stocks continue to fall amid AI jitters. Here's latest share prices of IT stocks.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: HDFC Bank, Canara Bank to PNB: These Bank Nifty majors remain in red even as index rebounds 1%; SBI, IDFC First lead
articles.summary: The banking index slipped 0.6% to an intraday low of 53,829.40 before recovering nearly 1% from the day's low to touch an intraday high of 54,333.70.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: HAL, BEL to Bharat Dynamics: Defence stocks rise despite weak trends in the Indian stock market
articles.summary: After rising over 1% during the last two trading sessions, the Nifty India Defence index added another 1.1% to touch the day's high of 9,131, taking its three-day rise to 2.2%. In the 19-pack index, 17 defence stocks rose, and only two declined, signalling broader optimism in the sector.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Anthropic offers Mythos to 150 additional groups; India to get AI model access
articles.summary: Anthropic broadens access to cybersecurity AI model Mythos via Project Glasswing for over 150 firms globally  to address critical software vulnerabilities. The initiative includes countries from Five Eyes alliance and Europe, signaling a trend of govt and businesses leveraging AI for cyber defe
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Oil prices fall amid US-Iran peace talks hopes; Brent at $97. Where are prices headed?
articles.summary: US-Iran war: MCX Crude oil prices fell 1.26% to ₹9,124 per barrel. Here's what's driving crude oil prices, along with experts' opinions on near-term outlook.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Drone stock ideaForge share price hits 5% upper circuit, 52-week high as board approves ₹500 crore fundraise
articles.summary: Drone stock ideaForge Technology said that the fundraising may be executed through various routes, including preferential allotment, private placement, qualified institutional placement (QIP) or any other permissible method under applicable laws.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: CMR Green Technologies IPO Day 2 LIVE: Issue booked 6.96x so far — Should you apply as GMP at 31%?
articles.summary: CMR Green Technologies IPO Day 2 LIVE: The ₹630.88-crore issue is entirely an offer for sale (OFS), with no fresh issue component. Under the IPO, promoters and an investor shareholder will sell up to 3.28 crore equity shares.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Gold rate today: MCX gold price rises above ₹1.59 lakh per 10 grams, silver gains amid US-Iran peace deal hopes
articles.summary: Gold rate today: Gold prices rose in the international market, supported by lower crude oil prices and a weaker dollar. Spot gold price gained 0.7% to $4,461.09 per ounce.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Rupee opens 1 paise higher at 95.70 against US dollar
articles.summary: Rupee opened 1 paise higher at 95.70 against the US dollar. Experts believe that yesterday, the rupee found itself standing in the middle of yet another global storm. The currency weakened towards 95.85, as a fresh wave of geopolitical tensions swept through financial markets.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Silver rate today rises as dollar weakens, oil prices retreat amid US-Iran peace hopes
articles.summary: MCX silver rate was flat but in the green, It rose 0.3% to ₹2,63,711 per kg. Meanwhile, MCX gold price was flat at ₹1,54,332 per 10 grams
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Rajesh Exports share price falls 5% after SEBI flags ₹15 lakh crore misstatement in accounts
articles.summary: The SEBI has accused Rajesh Exports of misrepresenting nearly all of its revenue over a five-year period, raising doubts on the reliability of the jewellery manufacturer's financial disclosures.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Indiabulls share price at 52-week high after firm raises ₹1000 crore through issuance of convertible warrants
articles.summary: Indiabulls plans to issue up to 51.55 crore warrants at an issue price of ₹19.40 per warrant, including a premium of ₹17.40 per share. Each warrant will be convertible into one fully paid-up equity share of the company.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Block Deals: Lenskart Solutions, GMR Airports shares to be in focus
articles.summary: Block Deals: SVF II Lightbulb (Cayman), an investment vehicle owned by SoftBank Group, sold 5.65 crore Lenskart shares, representing a 3.25% stake in the company at ₹508.55 apiece, taking the total deal value to ₹2,873.30 crore.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Mint Explainer | Why has the US proposed fresh tariffs on India?
articles.summary: Experts have argued that India should not fall for the pressure tactics. It should keep the latest Section 301 tariffs and bilateral trade agreements separate.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Crude falls over 1% as Israel-Lebanon ceasefire revives hopes of wider peace
articles.summary: Oil prices fell more than 1% after Israel and Lebanon agreed to renew a ceasefire, easing concerns over supply disruptions and lifting hopes of wider regional peace.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Cabinet clears ₹20,000 cr schemes for jet fuel support, cleaner Delhi vehicles
articles.summary: One-time budgetary support of up to ₹10,000 crore for OMCs to provide ATF price stabilization amid high crude prices. The budgetary support will be in the form of interest-free advances to OMCs.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: How have crude oil prices behaved during the West Asia war?
articles.summary: The West Asia war pushed Brent crude from about $70 to a peak of $126 per barrel, raising fuel costs and inflation in India, which imports 90% of its oil. Prices have eased amid Iran-US peace talks but remain elevated, keeping pressure on growth, consumers and businesses.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India-US trade deal weeks away as negotiators resolve 99% of issues, says US Ambassador Sergio Gor
articles.summary: US Ambassador Sergio Gor said negotiators have resolved 99% of outstanding issues and expect an interim trade agreement within weeks, with only technical matters left to be finalized.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India’s services sector maintains growth momentum in May as output expands: HSBC India Services PMI
articles.summary: The HSBC India Services Purchasing Managers’ Index, compiled by S&P Global, rose to 59.8 in May from 58.8 in April. It has continued to grow since April, after registering its weakest growth in 14 months in March.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Centre’s domestic financing nearly doubles to ₹3.6 trillion in April, easing capital spending pressure
articles.summary: Strong small savings inflows and residual domestic financing provide a cushion for the government's FY27 spending, reducing its reliance on market borrowings.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Why India's fertility problem may be worse than what headline figures suggest
articles.summary: Driven by changing societal aspirations, India's fertility rate has plunged below replacement levels, particularly in southern states. The desire to have more children is even less.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India GDP growth likely slowed to 7.3% in Q4 FY26: Mint poll
articles.summary: Domestic demand and government spending likely kept growth resilient in the January-March quarter despite headwinds from the West Asia war.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Govt trims fertilizer demand estimate after IMD downgrades monsoon forecast
articles.summary: Stocks currently stand at 19.98 million tonnes, more than 52% of the revised seasonal requirement, significantly ahead of the typical 33% benchmark at this time in the season.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Industrial output grows 4.9% in April as India shifts to new IIP series with 2022-23 base
articles.summary: The update marks the first official reading under a revised base year, which adds high-tech products and energy-related services to better reflect the modern economy.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India’s manufacturing PMI climbs to 55.0 in May as output hits three-month high
articles.summary: Driven by domestic demand resilience, Indian factory activity outperformed estimates in May as new orders and production grew at their fastest pace since February despite persistent cost pressures.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: GST collections rise 3.2% to ₹1.94 trillion in May
articles.summary: Excluding the impact of a one-time ₹10,000-crore GST payment made by a telecom operator for spectrum allocation in May 2025, gross collections recorded an adjusted growth of 9% during May
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Powell says Fed will lose credibility if president removes officials over policy differences
articles.summary: The remarks were his first since stepping down as Federal Reserve chair.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Govt to help 150,000 farmers access machinery, curb stubble burning
articles.summary: The government aims to support 150,000 small farmers with ₹8,550 crore for farm equipment access, promoting mechanisation and reducing stubble burning through the establishment of custom hiring centres, enhancing productivity and resource efficiency in agriculture.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Rain, thunderstorm seen in many parts, conditions favour monsoon progress: IMD
articles.summary: The IMD has forecast heavy rain in parts of northeast and south India, along with severe thunderstorms, hailstorms and winds up to 90 kmph across several regions. As heatwave conditions ease, southwest monsoon is expected to advance further over Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Lakshadweep and adjoining seas.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Mint Explainer | Is 2026 heading for its driest monsoon since 2015?
articles.summary: IMD has lowered its monsoon outlook to 90% of normal, raising concerns over heatwaves, farm output, and food prices as El Niño risks loom large.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: How the stress is building in real estate globally, explained in five charts
articles.summary: After soaring in the wake of the covid pandemic, real estate prices globally have been cooling down. The trickle-down effects of the US-Iran war could prolong that.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Govt warns of fresh inflation risks: Fuel prices, monsoon to further squeeze your household budget
articles.summary: The finance ministry's report comes just days ahead of RBI’s Monetary Policy decision scheduled to be announced next week.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India eases fuel export levies, lowers duties on petrol, diesel and ATF starting June 1
articles.summary: The duty on exports of petrol has been set at ₹1.5 ($0.0158) per litre while that on diesel has been set at ₹13.5 per litre. Export duties on ATF have been set at ₹9.5 per litre.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Will AI put economists out of business? Fed’s John Williams says 'I’m not a believer that...'
articles.summary: The implications of artificial intelligence gripped global central bankers in Iceland this week, with one Federal Reserve official joking that it won’t put economists out of business.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: The week in charts: Households’ health bills, emerging markets tracker, childcare gap
articles.summary: In this weekly Plain Facts compilation, we present to you data-based insights, with easy-to-read charts, to help you delve deeper into the stories reported by Mint in the week gone by.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India opens CSR funding to non-profits via social stock exchange in landmark policy shift
articles.summary: India government has allowed companies to invest up to 10 per cent of their CSR funds in zero coupon zero principal instruments issued by not for profit organisations through a social stock exchange
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: RBI balance sheet jumps 20.6% on gold rally, rupee depreciation
articles.summary: Liquidity infusion through RBI’s open market purchases of government securities was also a factor for the expansion in the balance sheet
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: RBI says West Asia war clouds growth outlook, but India remains relatively resilient
articles.summary: RBI cautioned that a prolonged escalation of the war could hurt growth momentum and rekindle inflationary pressures through surging crude oil prices, volatile commodity markets and disruptions in shipping routes.
articles.label: BEARISH
